mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines across 3-year rolling windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when the economy operates below full capacity.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 20-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4%, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60-day SPX returns, or the interaction effect fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day SPX direction, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand and supply chain disruptions, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO falls > 1.0%, or the threshold signal loses predictive power during contraction phases.
